articles.title: PCOS and Weight Changes: What Really Matters
articles.summary: Learn why PCOS-related weight changes are about hormones and metabolism—not willpower—and which treatments actually help.
articles.body:
Many people with PCOS notice weight gain or difficulty losing weight even with careful eating and exercise. This is often due to insulin resistance, androgen levels, and how PCOS affects metabolism.

In this guide, we explain how hormones influence weight, what lab tests can help identify root causes, and which evidence-based treatments can make a difference—including nutrition strategies, movement, sleep, stress management, and medications when appropriate.

We also discuss realistic expectations, why the scale is only one measure of health, and how to work with a hormone-focused clinician to build a sustainable plan.

articles.title: PCOS, Cravings, and Blood Sugar: How to Stabilize Your Day
articles.summary: Understand why PCOS can amplify cravings and how to use balanced meals to support more stable energy and mood.
articles.body:
PCOS is closely linked with insulin resistance, which can cause blood sugar swings and intense cravings. In this article, we break down what happens after you eat, how insulin works, and why PCOS may change your hunger cues.

We offer practical meal-building tips, snack ideas, and guidance on timing carbohydrates, protein, and fats to keep you feeling steady throughout the day.

articles.title: PCOS and Fertility: Basics of Ovulation and Treatment Options
articles.summary: An overview of how PCOS affects ovulation and the most common treatment paths for improving fertility.
articles.body:
PCOS is one of the leading causes of ovulatory infertility—but it is also very treatable. We explain how ovulation normally works, what changes in PCOS, and the tests your clinician may recommend.

You’ll learn about lifestyle approaches, medications like letrozole and clomiphene, and when to consider reproductive endocrinology referral.

articles.title: PCOS, Acne, and Hair Changes: Understanding Androgens
articles.summary: Why excess androgens with PCOS can cause acne and unwanted hair growth—and what can help.
articles.body:
Androgens—often called 'male hormones' though everyone has them—play an important role in PCOS symptoms. Elevated androgens can show up as acne, oily skin, scalp hair thinning, or excess hair on the face and body.

We review treatment options including spironolactone, combined hormonal contraceptives, topical treatments, and lifestyle strategies.

articles.title: Thyroid Labs 101: TSH, Free T4, and More
articles.summary: A clear explanation of common thyroid lab tests and how they relate to symptoms like fatigue and weight change.
articles.body:
Thyroid blood tests can be confusing. In this article, we explain TSH, free T4, free T3, thyroid antibodies, and reverse T3, and how they can relate to symptoms such as fatigue, weight gain, depression, and hair loss.

We also clarify why lab reference ranges differ from optimal symptom ranges for some people and discuss when treatment may be considered.

articles.title: Perimenopause and Sleep Problems: A Step-by-Step Plan
articles.summary: Why perimenopause disrupts sleep—and practical steps, from lifestyle changes to hormone therapy, that can help.
articles.body:
Falling estrogen and progesterone during perimenopause can lead to night sweats, early awakenings, and restless sleep. We explore the biology behind these changes and walk through a layered approach to better sleep.

This includes sleep hygiene, cognitive-behavioral tools, non-hormonal medications, and when hormone therapy may be appropriate.

articles.title: Managing Hot Flashes in Menopause: From Lifestyle to Hormone Therapy
articles.summary: An evidence-based guide to reducing hot flashes and night sweats during menopause.
articles.body:
Hot flashes and night sweats, also called vasomotor symptoms, are among the most common menopause complaints. We review triggers, tracking tools, lifestyle strategies, non-hormonal medications, and hormone therapy options.

We also explain safety data, including cardiovascular and breast health considerations.

articles.title: Testosterone Therapy: What to Expect in the First 3 Months
articles.summary: A timeline of expected changes, lab monitoring, and side effects during the first three months of testosterone therapy.
articles.body:
When starting testosterone, many people wonder how quickly they will notice changes in energy, libido, mood, and body composition. We outline a typical timeline, what is normal vs. concerning, and which labs your clinician may check.

We also cover injection vs. gel or patch options, dose adjustments, and safety monitoring.

articles.title: Estrogen Patch Side Effects: What’s Normal and What’s Not
articles.summary: Learn which estrogen patch side effects are common, which are red flags, and when to message your care team.
articles.body:
Transdermal estrogen patches are a convenient way to treat menopausal and perimenopausal symptoms. Mild headaches, breast tenderness, or skin irritation can occur when starting therapy.

We walk through common and less common side effects, how long they typically last, and when it’s important to contact your care team urgently—especially for severe headaches, chest pain, leg swelling, or visual changes.

articles.title: How Hormones Affect Weight Across Your Lifespan
articles.summary: From puberty to menopause and beyond, hormones shape how your body stores and uses energy.
articles.body:
Hormones like estrogen, progesterone, testosterone, insulin, cortisol, and thyroid hormone all influence body weight, fat distribution, and muscle mass.

In this article, we walk through key life stages—puberty, reproductive years, pregnancy, postpartum, perimenopause, and menopause—and explain how changing hormone levels affect metabolism and weight.

articles.title: Your First Thyroid Video Visit: What to Expect
articles.summary: How to prepare for a virtual thyroid appointment, from lab results to symptom tracking.
articles.body:
Virtual thyroid visits can be just as effective as in-person appointments for many people. We outline what information to gather ahead of time, how to track symptoms, and which questions to ask about labs, medications, and follow-up.

You’ll also learn how to use our platform’s video tools, secure messaging, and lab integrations.

articles.title: Perimenopause and Mood Changes: When to Seek Support
articles.summary: Understand the link between hormonal shifts and mood, and how to get the right support.
articles.body:
Fluctuating hormone levels can worsen anxiety, irritability, and depression during perimenopause. We describe common patterns, screening tools, and treatment approaches ranging from therapy and lifestyle changes to medications and hormone therapy.

We also highlight warning signs that warrant urgent care.

care_recommendations.title: Fast virtual visit within 3 days for perimenopause symptoms
care_recommendations.summary: Connect with a hormone specialist by video within 3 days to address hot flashes, night sweats, and sleep problems.
care_recommendations.description:
Based on your symptoms and goals, we recommend a fast-track virtual visit with a clinician experienced in perimenopause. You’ll review your symptom history, discuss lifestyle changes and treatment options, and decide whether hormone therapy or non-hormonal options fit your needs.

Most patients complete this visit within 3 days and leave with a clear plan.

care_recommendations.title: Comprehensive hormone optimization plan with quarterly check-ins
care_recommendations.summary: A structured treatment plan with regular virtual visits, lab monitoring, and messaging support.
care_recommendations.description: This recommendation includes an initial deep-dive visit, personalized lab panel, and quarterly virtual follow-ups to adjust treatment. Ideal if you want ongoing optimization for energy, mood, libido, and body composition.

care_recommendations.title: Perimenopause sleep reset self-care plan
care_recommendations.summary: Step-by-step non-medication strategies to improve sleep quality.
care_recommendations.description: A self-guided program that covers sleep hygiene, evening routines, light exposure, and relaxation techniques tailored to hormonal sleep disruption. Includes printable trackers and guided audio exercises.

care_recommendations.title: At-home baseline hormone lab panel
care_recommendations.summary: Collect labs at home to better understand your hormone levels before or during treatment.
care_recommendations.description: You’ll receive an at-home collection kit for key hormones such as estradiol, progesterone, testosterone, SHBG, and thyroid markers when appropriate. Results are reviewed with a clinician and used to personalize your care plan.

care_recommendations.title: Thyroid video visit with optional at-home lab kit
care_recommendations.summary: Meet with a thyroid-focused clinician by video and add labs if needed.
care_recommendations.description: Ideal for fatigue, weight changes, or known thyroid conditions. We review your history and existing labs, then recommend any additional testing and a treatment plan.

care_recommendations.title: PCOS metabolic reset plan
care_recommendations.summary: A combined lifestyle and medication approach for insulin resistance and weight-related PCOS symptoms.
care_recommendations.description: This plan pairs nutrition coaching, movement guidance, and medication options such as metformin when appropriate. Includes messaging access to your care team between visits.

care_recommendations.title: Testosterone safety check and dose review
care_recommendations.summary: Focused video visit to review side effects, labs, and dosing of testosterone therapy.
care_recommendations.description: Recommended if you’re on testosterone and experiencing new symptoms or lab changes. Your clinician will review your regimen, side effects, and goals, and may adjust your dose or schedule follow-up labs.

care_recommendations.title: Menopause hormone therapy review within 7 days
care_recommendations.summary: Quick-access virtual visit to fine-tune your estrogen and progesterone plan.
care_recommendations.description: If you’re on or considering hormone therapy for menopause, this visit focuses on symptom control, side effects, and safety. We’ll review your current regimen and discuss adjustments.

medications.name: Testosterone cypionate 100mg/mL
medications.dosage_instructions_default: Inject 0.5 mL intramuscularly every 7 days or as directed by your clinician.

medications.name: Estrogen patch 0.05 mg
medications.dosage_instructions_default: Apply one patch to clean, dry skin twice weekly. Rotate sites to avoid irritation.

medications.name: Levothyroxine 50 mcg tablet
medications.dosage_instructions_default: Take 1 tablet by mouth once daily on an empty stomach, 30–60 minutes before breakfast.

medications.name: Progesterone 100mg capsule
medications.dosage_instructions_default: Take 1–2 capsules by mouth at bedtime as directed, often 12 days per month or daily.

medications.name: Metformin ER 500mg tablet
medications.dosage_instructions_default: Take 1 tablet with the evening meal. Your dose may be increased gradually as directed.

medications.name: Spironolactone 50mg tablet
medications.dosage_instructions_default: Take 1 tablet by mouth once or twice daily as directed. Take with food if stomach upset occurs.

medications.name: Estradiol transdermal gel 0.75 mg
medications.dosage_instructions_default: Apply the prescribed amount to clean, dry skin of the upper arm once daily. Allow to dry before dressing.

medications.name: Vitamin D3 2000 IU capsule
medications.dosage_instructions_default: Take 1 capsule by mouth daily with food, or as directed based on your vitamin D level.

medications.name: DHEA 25mg capsule
medications.dosage_instructions_default: Take 1 capsule by mouth in the morning, or as directed after lab review.

medications.name: Testosterone 1% gel
medications.dosage_instructions_default: Apply the prescribed amount to clean, dry skin of the shoulders or upper arms once daily. Wash hands after application.

medications.name: Estradiol 1mg tablet
medications.dosage_instructions_default: Take 1 tablet by mouth once daily with or without food, as directed.

providers.bio: Dr. Wong is a board-certified endocrinologist with a focus on thyroid disorders and complex hormone cases. She has over 10 years of telemedicine experience.
Dr. Desai specializes in PCOS, metabolic health, and fertility-related hormone care.
Laura is a nurse practitioner focused on perimenopause and menopause, with special interest in sleep and mood symptoms.
Dr. Carter provides testosterone management and long-term hormone optimization for adults of all genders.
Dr. King focuses on complex menopausal symptom management and mood changes around midlife.
Dr. Lin bridges PCOS, thyroid, and metabolic care, emphasizing realistic lifestyle changes.
Dana is a registered dietitian specializing in PCOS-related nutrition and metabolic health.
Dr. Kapoor focuses on PCOS, acne, and hair changes related to hormones.
Anthony is a nurse practitioner providing thyroid follow-up and dose adjustments via telehealth.
Dr. Martinez focuses on menopause care, including hormone and non-hormone options.
Dr. Lee manages ongoing hormone follow-up visits, focusing on fine-tuning treatment plans.

symptoms.name: Hot flashes
symptoms.description: Sudden waves of heat, often with flushing and sweating, that may last several minutes.

symptoms.name: Night sweats
symptoms.description: Episodes of intense sweating during sleep that may soak clothing or bedding.

symptoms.name: Sleep problems
symptoms.description: Difficulty falling asleep, staying asleep, or waking too early.

symptoms.name: Headache
symptoms.description: Pain or pressure in the head, which can range from mild to severe.

symptoms.name: Nausea
symptoms.description: A feeling of sickness or queasiness that may be accompanied by an urge to vomit.

symptoms.name: Fatigue
symptoms.description: A persistent sense of tiredness or lack of energy that is not relieved by rest.

symptoms.name: Mood changes
symptoms.description: Changes in mood such as irritability, sadness, anxiety, or mood swings.

symptoms.name: Other symptom
symptoms.description: Any symptom not listed in the main catalog; additional details are entered in free text.

visit_reasons.description: Evaluation and management of hypothyroidism, hyperthyroidism, Hashimoto’s, and related concerns.
Consults focused on PCOS symptoms such as irregular periods, acne, hair changes, and metabolic health.
Care for symptoms during the transition leading up to menopause, including hot flashes and sleep changes.
Management of menopausal symptoms and long-term hormone health.
Shorter visits to review progress, adjust doses, and review lab results for existing patients.
Initial or general consultation to discuss hormone-related symptoms and possible treatment options.

refill_requests.decision_note: Approved and electronically sent to Market Street Community Pharmacy.
Approved for a 30-day refill; patient notified in portal.
Refill denied pending updated thyroid labs; message sent to patient with instructions.

prescriptions.dosage_instructions: Inject 0.5 mL intramuscularly every 7 days, or as directed by your clinician.
Apply one 0.05 mg patch to clean, dry skin twice weekly. Rotate sites with each new patch.
Take 1 tablet (50 mcg) by mouth every morning on an empty stomach, 30–60 minutes before breakfast.
Take 2 capsules (200 mg) by mouth at bedtime for 12 days each month or as directed.
Take 1 tablet (500 mg) with the evening meal for 1 week, then increase to 2 tablets with the evening meal as tolerated.
Take 1 tablet (50 mg) by mouth twice daily with food, or as directed.
Apply the prescribed amount of gel to clean, dry skin of the upper arm once daily in the morning. Allow to dry before covering.
Take 1 capsule (2000 IU) by mouth daily with food.
Take 1 capsule (25 mg) by mouth each morning, or as directed after lab review.
Apply the prescribed amount of gel to clean, dry skin of the shoulders or upper arms once daily in the morning.
Take 1 tablet (1 mg) by mouth once daily.
